public_reputation: Scribe preserving funerary sequences, divine mediation records, and sacred scheduling inside Panim rites.
appearance: Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul carries the marks of ritual scribe in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries the habits of a ritual scribe whose work is visible through daily procedure, gatekeeping, and the practical pressures of Panim Haven.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of ritual scribe in and around Panim Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Panim Haven while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul can draw the party into a practical problem centered on Panim Haven.
Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul becomes useful when the party needs access, explanation, or relief tied to the work of a ritual scribe.

support_scope_notes: Created from the estate and daily-life infrastructure execution patch to make polity-native estate operations usable at table scale.
public_signal: In public, Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul is readable as a ritual scribe tied to Panim Haven.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Panim Haven: role-relevant halls, counters, corridors, docks, archives, shrines, workshops, gates, or administrative rooms

first_impression: Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul presents as someone shaped by the duties of a ritual scribe and the daily pressures of Panim Haven.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a ritual scribe, and proof that strangers will not make Panim Haven harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a ritual scribe too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul can open local doors, explain expectations, and redirect pressure before it hardens.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul always knows more about Panim Haven than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to ritual scribe duties in Panim Haven.
social_texture: Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Panim Haven, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Ritual touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Ritual Scribe Maresa Ul when the party needs a socially grounded face for Panim Haven: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
